title: cacosmia rugosa var. kingii
intro: Cacosmia rugosa var. kingii, also known as King's Cacosmia or Cacosmia Kingii, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of New South Wales and Victoria in Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Cacosmia rugosa var. kingii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of Cacosmia rugosa var. kingii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
